name: Seraulim
notes: The Google Plus code uses Mungul, as does the What's Here option in the context menu.  Seraulim and Mungul seem to be almost (but not quite) identical regions/districts that overalp almost exactly.  I took the v2 scripts default (Seraulim) as it appeard as correct as the Google Maps result (Mungul) so wasn't worth figuring out the difference; both are just as usable for my purposes and I wouldn't consider it an error if future interations of my script flipped this and used Mungul

name: Boltaña

Anyway, this seems to be a good level to work with, at least in rural areas.  I don't know if other areas of Spain use the same structure.  Happy to discuss

name: Goya
notes: Goya is a fairly small region of the huge city of Madrid.  The next administrative region up is Salamanca but I think in big cities I like the local administrative neighborhood (like I do in Canberra, where the suburb not the region is best).  This example does not reflect a current location in my eBird records, but I wanted to see what a non-rural location might look like.  I have not checked other Spanish cities.  Happy to discuss.
